facts.text: Freeport earned its name because boats could dock there without paying fees at the junction of Four Mile Creek and Lafayette Creek.
The town began as Genoa and later Four Mile Landing, its port on Choctawhatchee Bay drawing settlers in the 1830s.
When the railroad arrived in DeFuniak Springs, Freeport’s maritime trade fell, shifting county power to the rail hub.
Eglin Air Force Base’s mid‑20th‑century expansion to the east brought new residents and economic opportunity to Freeport.
Today Freeport blends historic small‑town charm with rapid growth, earning a spot among Florida’s fastest‑growing cities.
Freeport’s resilience shows in its modern identity: a former shipping port now a connector between Walton County’s north and south, honoring its “free port” heritage.
